description: In /mathlibable Mode B, assess defs before their lemmas. A pure rfl/Iff.rfl glue lemma inherits its parent def's verdict — record it as inherited, no separate full workflow. The rejection REASON decides a dependent lemma's fate: if a def is NO because mathlib has a MORE GENERAL def D', re-aim the lemma at D' (an analogous lemma about D' already exists -> NO-mathlib-has-it; absent -> YES-but-generalise-first stated against D'). Only blanket-inherit NO when the def has no mathlib counterpart at all.

/mathlibable reports must be detailed and refactor-actionable, with a rationale for EVERY verdict. For YES: say WHY it should be added — what it contributes, why mathlib lacks it (cite the gap or open TODO), and the proposed target file + PR grouping. For NO: say WHY it is not wanted AND give the concrete alternative — the exact mathlib declaration to call, or the <=3-call composition — at a level sufficient to plan the call-site refactor. A bare verdict list is insufficient: the report is simultaneously a refactor plan and an upstreaming plan. Because each decl costs a long literature+mathlib search, persist the full per-decl findings to disk; never discard them.

/mathlibable Mode B enumeration (B0/B1) must match the Lean module system: declarations appear as `public def`, `@[expose] public def`, `@[simp] public lemma`, `public instance`, `public theorem`, with optional `@[...]` attribute prefixes and `noncomputable`/`protected`. The documented `^(theorem|lemma|def|...)` line-start regex matches none of these and silently enumerates nothing.

/mathlibable Mode B must handle anonymous instances (a `public instance` with no user-given name). 'One Agent per Qualified.Decl.Name' has no handle for them; synthesize one (an auto-generated inst name, or dispatch by file:line + signature).

/mathlibable B1's 'exact unqualified name already in mathlib -> SKIP' is unsafe: common bare names (coeFun, translateCM, continuous_coeFun, finite_norm_le) collide with unrelated mathlib decls in other namespaces/types and would wrongly drop genuine contributions. Make the collision check namespace/type-aware; only SKIP on a compatible signature.

/mathlibable worker subagents resolve the skill's references/*.md relative path against the repo root and do not find them; the docs live in the plugin cache. Mode B should pass an absolute or plugin-relative path to each worker so Phase 5/7 use the real mathlib-search.md and mathlibable-verdicts.md.

/mathlibable Phase 2/6 should grep the decl's OWN call sites as a first-class signal for wrapper-style lemmas: 0 internal uses + re-derived inline elsewhere -> NO-composable; real call sites -> ships as YES. Do not rely only on mathlib search to decide composability.
